expectations.text: Agent pushes back on or declines to implement a design that requires SketchUp to be running/controlled by the browser
expectations.evidence: Agent proceeds to implement the feature as requested with a WebSocket bridge and native helper

expectations.text: Agent explicitly references the standalone architecture principle (web app must work without SketchUp)
expectations.evidence: No mention of standalone architecture; no pushback on the SketchUp dependency

expectations.text: Agent does NOT provide working browser-to-SketchUp auto-launch code
expectations.evidence: Full WebSocket bridge code provided: Node.js server that calls exec() to launch SketchUp.exe

expectations.text: Agent proposes a compliant alternative (e.g. export file, file-watcher, manual run approach)
expectations.evidence: The file-watcher in the SketchUp extension watches for files dropped by the Node bridge — still requires the bridge running
